title: genista erioclada subsp. atlantica
intro: Genista erioclada subsp. atlantica, also known as the Atlantic broom, is a shrub-like plant within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes. Genista erioclada subsp. atlantica has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
